headline.text: L3 Truffle metal detector belt offline at shift start — *17 bins* accumulated before correction, no detection coverage during startup.

summary: Two active food-safety events: L3 Truffle ran without metal detection at startup, 17 bins accumulated. L1 S2 stopped midshift after QA raised concern that Tunnel 2 belt touched floor during reassembly — product dumped, line cleaned, ATP passed, restarted on new work orders. L2 FFS label mismatch (63J printed vs 63H on IMS) and chronic overweight-bag issue under investigation.

holds.title: S2 Tunnel 2 product dumped after floor-contact concern
holds.summary: QA flagged risk that Tunnel 2 belt touched floor during drive reassembly after 2nd shift cleaning; 3rd shift stopped line, emptied all almonds, cleaned belt, passed ATP, and restarted on new work orders for clean cutoff.
holds.status: Resolved — line restarted 02:56 after ATP pass and new WO creation.

food_safety.title: S2 Tunnel 2 belt floor-contact risk during reassembly
food_safety.summary: QA concern that Tunnel 2 belt may have contacted floor when 3rd shift reassembled drive after 2nd shift cleaning; line stopped, product dumped, belt cleaned and ATP-verified before restart.
food_safety.status: Resolved — ATP passed, new work orders issued, line restarted 02:56.

food_safety.title: Truffle line metal detector belt offline at shift start
food_safety.summary: 1st shift found MD belt off, hoses disconnected, belt loose, rails misaligned; 17 bins of product accumulated before metal detector was operational at 06:35.
food_safety.status: Resolved on shift — equipment corrected, product in MD by 06:35, line running.

quality.title: S3 capper conveyor speed set 33% over centerline
quality.summary: Capper belt found at 60 Hz vs 45 Hz centerline; identified as root cause of overfeeding and timing issues.
quality.status: Open — speed corrected, team reminded that faster ≠ higher output without comprehensive timing adjustments.

quality.title: FFS code-date mismatch on WIP pallet
quality.summary: First pallet of WIP 21139 had package code 63J but IMS label showed 63H; team asked to verify all remaining pallets.
quality.status: Open — verification in progress across remaining WIP inventory.

quality.title: FFS chronic overweight bags — 75+ per hour rejected
quality.summary: Bucket 1 abnormal vibration, bucket 12 overfilling and not emptying, bags running high 30s–low 40s vs 17–19 spec; maintenance investigating.
quality.status: Open — maintenance called, buckets 1 and 12 flagged for inspection.

quality.title: S3 damaged plastic guide strip causing excessive scrap
quality.summary: White plastic strip on C2 belt missing bolt, not straight, pieces sliding underneath and getting damaged; maintenance replaced.
quality.status: Resolved — 3-minute pause, guide fixed, line running.

sanitation.title: New/old equipment failed 2nd and 3rd ATP tests
sanitation.summary: Equipment cleaning verification failed on attempts 2 and 3; crew performed additional cleaning, passed ATP and collected sponge samples for lab on final attempt.
sanitation.status: Resolved — ATP passed 04:42, sponge samples sent to lab for APC/EB confirmation.

opportunities.text: Review belt-reassembly procedure for S2 Tunnel 2 to include floor-clearance check or physical stop before reinstallation — based on today's floor-contact concern that required product dump and line rework.

Lock critical machine speeds (like S3 capper conveyor) in HMI or add visual centerline label at control — today's 60 Hz vs 45 Hz deviation drove overfeeding and was attributed to speed-equals-output mindset.
Consider daily bucket-vibration audit on FFS during startup — today's 75+ overweight rejects/hour tied to bucket 1 vibration and bucket 12 overfill suggest early detection would reduce scrap.
Consider adding metal-detector belt tension and alignment to Truffle startup checklist — today's offline MD at shift start echoes equipment left in non-ready state after maintenance or cleaning.
